lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਐਸੋ ਰੇ ਭੇਟੈ ਸੰਤੁ ਮੇਰੀ ਲਾਹੈ ਸਗਲ ਚਿੰਤ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਸਿਉ ਮੇਰਾ ਰੰਗੁ ਲਾਵੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: koee aiso re bhettai sant meree laahai sagal chint tthaakur siau meraa rang laavai |2|
lines.english: Is there any such Saint, who would meet with me, take away my anxiety, and lead me to enshrine love for my Lord and Master. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜੇ ਰੇ ਸਗਲ ਬੇਦ ਨਹ ਚੂਕੈ ਮਨ ਭੇਦ ਇਕੁ ਖਿਨੁ ਨ ਧੀਰਹਿ ਮੇਰੇ ਘਰ ਕੇ ਪੰਚਾ ॥
lines.translit: parre re sagal bed neh chookai man bhed ik khin na dheereh mere ghar ke panchaa |
lines.english: I have read all the Vedas, and yet the sense of separation in my mind still has not been removed; the five thieves of my house are not quieted, even for an instant.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਤੇ ਰੇ ਤੀਰਥ ਨਾਏ ਅਹੰਬੁਧਿ ਮੈਲੁ ਲਾਏ ਘਰ ਕੋ ਠਾਕੁਰੁ ਇਕੁ ਤਿਲੁ ਨ ਮਾਨੈ ॥
lines.translit: jete re teerath naae ahanbudh mail laae ghar ko tthaakur ik til na maanai |
lines.english: In spite of the many places of pilgrimage for people to bathe in, their minds are still stained by their stubborn ego; the Lord Master is not pleased by this at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਐਸੋ ਰੇ ਭਗਤੁ ਜੁ ਮਾਇਆ ਤੇ ਰਹਤੁ ਇਕੁ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਮੇਰੈ ਰਿਦੈ ਸਿੰਚਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: koee aiso re bhagat ju maaeaa te rehat ik amrit naam merai ridai sinchaa |3|
lines.english: Is there any devotee, who is unattached to Maya, who may irrigate my mind with the Ambrosial Naam, the Name of the One Lord? ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਦਿ ਪਾਵਉ ਸਾਧਸੰਗੁ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਸਦਾ ਆਨੰਦੁ ਗਿਆਨ ਅੰਜਨਿ ਮੇਰਾ ਮਨੁ ਇਸਨਾਨੈ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: kad paavau saadhasang har har sadaa aanand giaan anjan meraa man isanaanai |4|
lines.english: When will I find the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy? There, I shall be always in the ecstasy of the Lord, Har, Har, and my mind shall take its cleansing bath in the healing ointment of spiritual wisdom. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲ ਅਸ੍ਰਮ ਕੀਨੇ ਮਨੂਆ ਨਹ ਪਤੀਨੇ ਬਿਬੇਕਹੀਨ ਦੇਹੀ ਧੋਏ ॥
lines.translit: sagal asram keene manooaa neh pateene bibekaheen dehee dhoe |
lines.english: I have followed the four stages of life, but my mind is not satisfied; I wash my body, but it is totally lacking in understanding.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਪਾਈਐ ਰੇ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਬਿਧਾਤਾ ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਕੈ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਾਤਾ ਮੇਰੇ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਮਲੁ ਖੋਏ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: koee paaeeai re purakh bidhaataa paarabraham kai rang raataa mere man kee duramat mal khoe |5|
lines.english: If only I could meet some devotee of the Supreme Lord God, imbued with the Lord's Love, who could eradicate the filthy evil-mindedness from my mind. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਮ ਧਰਮ ਜੁਗਤਾ ਨਿਮਖ ਨ ਹੇਤੁ ਕਰਤਾ ਗਰਬਿ ਗਰਬਿ ਪੜੈ ਕਹੀ ਨ ਲੇਖੈ ॥
lines.translit: karam dharam jugataa nimakh na het karataa garab garab parrai kahee na lekhai |
lines.english: One who is attached to religious rituals, does not love the Lord, even for an instant; he is filled with pride, and he is of no account.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਭੇਟੀਐ ਸਫਲ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਕਰੈ ਸਦਾ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਕੋਊ ਨੇਤ੍ਰਹੁ ਪੇਖੈ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: jis bhetteeai safal moorat karai sadaa keerat gur parasaad koaoo netrahu pekhai |6|
lines.english: One who meets with the rewarding personality of the Guru, continually sings the Kirtan of the Lord's Praises. By Guru's Grace, such a rare one beholds the Lord with his eyes. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਹਠਿ ਜੋ ਕਮਾਵੈ ਤਿਲੁ ਨ ਲੇਖੈ ਪਾਵੈ ਬਗੁਲ ਜਿਉ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਲਾਵੈ ਮਾਇਆ ਰੇ ਧਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: manahatth jo kamaavai til na lekhai paavai bagul jiau dhiaan laavai maaeaa re dhaaree |
lines.english: One who acts through stubbornness is of no account at all; like a crane, he pretends to meditate, but he is still stuck in Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਐਸੋ ਰੇ ਸੁਖਹ ਦਾਈ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੀ ਕਥਾ ਸੁਨਾਈ ਤਿਸੁ ਭੇਟੇ ਗਤਿ ਹੋਇ ਹਮਾਰੀ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: koee aiso re sukhah daaee prabh kee kathaa sunaaee tis bhette gat hoe hamaaree |7|
lines.english: Is there any such Giver of peace, who can recite to me the sermon of God? Meeting him, I would be emancipated. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਪ੍ਰਸੰਨ ਗੋਪਾਲ ਰਾਇ ਕਾਟੈ ਰੇ ਬੰਧਨ ਮਾਇ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਮੇਰਾ ਮਨੁ ਰਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: suprasan gopaal raae kaattai re bandhan maae gur kai sabad meraa man raataa |
lines.english: When the Lord, my King, is totally pleased with me, He will break the bonds of Maya for me; my mind is imbued with the Word of the Guru's Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਸਦਾ ਆਨੰਦੁ ਭੇਟਿਓ ਨਿਰਭੈ ਗੋਬਿੰਦੁ ਸੁਖ ਨਾਨਕ ਲਾਧੇ ਹਰਿ ਚਰਨ ਪਰਾਤਾ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: sadaa sadaa aanand bhettio nirabhai gobind sukh naanak laadhe har charan paraataa |8|
lines.english: I am in ecstasy, forever and ever, meeting the Fearless Lord, the Lord of the Universe. Falling at the Lord's Feet, Nanak has found peace. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਫਲ ਸਫਲ ਭਈ ਸਫਲ ਜਾਤ੍ਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: safal safal bhee safal jaatraa |
lines.english: My Yatra, my life pilgrimage, has become fruitful, fruitful, fruitful.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣ ਰਹੇ ਮਿਲੇ ਸਾਧਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ਦੂਜਾ ॥੧॥੩॥
lines.translit: aavan jaan rahe mile saadhaa |1| rahaau doojaa |1|3|
lines.english: My comings and goings have ended, since I met the Holy Saint. ||1||Second Pause||1||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨਾਸਰੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਛੰਤ ॥
lines.translit: dhanaasaree mahalaa 1 chhant |
lines.english: Dhanaasaree, First Mehl, Chhant:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰਥੁ ਸਬਦ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: teerath sabad beechaar antar giaan hai |
lines.english: My sacred shrine of pilgrimage is spiritual wisdom within, and contemplation on the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਸਾਚਾ ਥਾਨੁ ਤੀਰਥੁ ਦਸ ਪੁਰਬ ਸਦਾ ਦਸਾਹਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: gur giaan saachaa thaan teerath das purab sadaa dasaaharaa |
lines.english: The spiritual wisdom given by the Guru is the True sacred shrine of pilgrimage, where the ten festivals are always observed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰਥਿ ਨਾਵਣ ਜਾਉ ਤੀਰਥੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: teerath naavan jaau teerath naam hai |
lines.english: Why should I bathe at sacred shrines of pilgrimage? The Naam, the Name of the Lord, is the sacred shrine of pilgrimage.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ਰੋਗੀ ਨਾਮੁ ਦਾਰੂ ਮੈਲੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਸਚ ਬਿਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: sansaar rogee naam daaroo mail laagai sach binaa |
lines.english: The world is sick, and the Naam is the medicine to cure it; without the True Lord, filth sticks to it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਨਾਮੁ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਸਦਾ ਜਾਚਉ ਦੇਹੁ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਧਰਣੀਧਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: hau naam har kaa sadaa jaachau dehu prabh dharaneedharaa |
lines.english: I constantly beg for the Name of the Lord; grant it to me, O God, Sustainer of the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਵਾਕੁ ਨਿਰਮਲੁ ਸਦਾ ਚਾਨਣੁ ਨਿਤ ਸਾਚੁ ਤੀਰਥੁ ਮਜਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: gur vaak niramal sadaa chaanan nit saach teerath majanaa |1|
lines.english: The Guru's Word is immaculate and pure; it radiates a steady Light. Constantly bathe in such a true shrine of pilgrimage. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚਿ ਨ ਲਾਗੈ ਮੈਲੁ ਕਿਆ ਮਲੁ ਧੋਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: saach na laagai mail kiaa mal dhoeeai |
lines.english: Filth does not stick to the true ones; what filth do they have to wash off?

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣਹਿ ਹਾਰੁ ਪਰੋਇ ਕਿਸ ਕਉ ਰੋਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: guneh haar paroe kis kau roeeai |
lines.english: If one strings a garland of virtues for oneself, what is there to cry for?

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੀਚਾਰਿ ਮਾਰੈ ਤਰੈ ਤਾਰੈ ਉਲਟਿ ਜੋਨਿ ਨ ਆਵਏ ॥
lines.translit: veechaar maarai tarai taarai ulatt jon na aave |
lines.english: One who conquers his own self through contemplation is saved, and saves others as well; he does not come to be born again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਨੰਦੁ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਹਰਖੁ ਸਾਚਾ ਦੂਖ ਕਿਲਵਿਖ ਪਰਹਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: aanand anadin harakh saachaa dookh kilavikh parahare |
lines.english: He is in ecstasy, truly happy, night and day; his sorrows and sins are taken away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਪਾਰਸੁ ਪਰਮ ਧਿਆਨੀ ਸਾਚੁ ਸਾਚੇ ਭਾਵਏ ॥
lines.translit: aap paaras param dhiaanee saach saache bhaave |
lines.english: The supreme meditator is Himself the philosopher's stone, which transforms lead into gold. The true man is pleasing to the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮੀਤ ਮਿਲਾਪੁ ਪੂਰਾ ਨਾਵਣੋ ॥
lines.translit: sangat meet milaap pooraa naavano |
lines.english: O friend, association with the Holy is the perfect cleansing bath.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਗੁਰਿ ਦਿਖਾਇਆ ਮੈਲੁ ਨਾਹੀ ਸਚ ਮਨੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sach naam paaeaa gur dikhaaeaa mail naahee sach mane |2|
lines.english: He finds the True Name, and beholds the Guru; with the True Name in his mind, no filth sticks to him. ||2||
